ukg-4x4-core.description: 4-on-floor UK garage production template with a club-weighted low end, front-half drum establishment, and two-drop release shape.

arrangement_sections.goal: Establish kick, tops, and one harmonic teaser without exposing the full hook.
Reveal the full groove and primary synth conversation.
Thin the drums, open the space, and set up the second drop.
Bring back the groove with one extra layer, stronger automation, and clearer hook focus.
Deconstruct back to drums and one support layer for a DJ-safe exit.

sample_slots.purpose: Ticking top-end groove that glues to the kick pattern.
Fills the groove between the kick and clap without stealing focus.
Adds motion, grit, or room around the programmed drums.
Reserved lane for chop, adlib, or a later vocal phrase.
Uplifter, downlifter, impact, or reverse texture used to mark section changes.
Background air that keeps the intro and break from feeling empty.

devices: EQ Eight for low-end cleanup or top-end shaping only where needed
Saturator for density at low dry/wet
Glue Compressor on the drum bus rather than on every one-shot
EQ Eight high-pass to keep the kick lane clean
Utility to set width intentionally
Short send to room reverb only if the loop feels too dry
EQ Eight high-pass to keep the loop out of the sub region
Auto Pan or sidechain tool if the loop crowds the kick
Saturator or Drum Buss for grit if the layer feels flat
Utility set mono-safe below the groove center
EQ Eight to trim excess low-mid bloom before bus saturation
Saturator or mild clipping for density
Sidechain compression or envelope shaping against the kick
EQ Eight high-pass above the true sub lane
Saturator to add bite
Compressor or sidechain to keep it behind the bass anchor
EQ Eight high-pass to protect the bass lane
Chorus/Ensemble or detune source for width if the patch is too narrow
Send to long reverb and shorter delay return
Sidechain or rhythmic ducking to keep the groove breathing
EQ Eight to clear low-end spill
Saturator or gentle drive if the hook lacks edge
Delay send for phrase tails
Controlled reverb so the hook stays in front of the pad
EQ Eight to carve out overlap with the main hook
Short reverb or room send for placement
Sidechain or envelope shaping if it clouds the backbeat
High-pass to stop the lane stealing body from the bass and chords
Delay and reverb sends only, unless the sample needs texture
Transient reduction or clip fading to keep it gluey
Filter automation for risers and downlifters
Reverb or delay throw printed into the section boundary
Utility gain ride to stop transitions peaking over the groove
High-pass filter so the bed lives above the rhythm section
Long reverb or texture delay
Subtle sidechain if the bed makes drops feel static

returns.name: Short Room
returns.purpose: Light glue for drums, stabs, and vocal chops.

returns.name: Long Hall
returns.purpose: Wash for pads, breakdowns, and atmosphere.

returns.name: Dub Delay
returns.purpose: Phrase-end throws on hooks and vocals.

automation_rules: Open filter or send level slightly into the break rather than automating every bar.
Reset most FX sends on the first bar of each drop so the groove re-enters dry and confident.
Use one obvious throw or transition per section change rather than filling every gap.
mix_rules: Only one true low_end_anchor should own the sub information at a time.
Pads and atmospheres must be high-passed and intentionally backgrounded.
Hooks should sit in front by tone and timing, not only by raw volume.
Leave one lane open for a later chop or one-shot if the arrangement feels overfilled.

export_plan.target_bounce: 24-bit WAV master and lesson reference bounce
export_plan.headroom_note: Keep enough pre-limiter headroom that the drop still breathes.
final_checks: Drop entry hits harder than the break
Transition FX do not peak above the hook
Outro is usable for a DJ mix-out

ukg-2step-core.description: 2-step UK garage production template with syncopated drum space, denser bass conversation, and darker midrange support.

arrangement_sections.goal: Introduce a trimmed version of the groove and one support layer while leaving the main stab hidden.
Expose the full 2-step rhythm, bass conversation, and main stab feature.
Strip out the heavy drums and widen the support layers before the second groove section.
Return with the same core groove plus one extra loop, response, or automation lift.
Create a short rhythmic left turn, half-time tease, or filtered reset.
Reduce back to kick skeleton, selected tops, and one support texture.

sample_slots.purpose: Anchor for syncopated drums and sidechain timing.
Defines the backbeat around the swing pocket.
Drives the shuffle and ties ghost placements together.
Adds rolling motion between the snare holes.
Answering rhythm or turnaround hit for the 2-step pocket.
Break or garage loop layer that adds movement without replacing the programmed groove.
Reserved lane for chopped vocal punctuation or call-and-response chop.
Dark bed or texture for the intro and break.

devices: EQ Eight cleanup and gain staging
Saturator or Drum Buss for body if the hit feels small
Glue Compressor only at the bus level
EQ Eight high-pass to stay out of the kick lane
Transient shaping or clip fades to keep the pocket tight
Optional room send for glue
High-pass filter to remove low-end spill
Duck or sidechain against the kick
Saturator if the loop needs dirt
Mono-safe Utility
EQ Eight notch if the stab and bass fight in low mids
Saturator or clipping for consistency
Sidechain envelope shaping against the kick
High-pass above the true sub range
Drive or chorus only if it helps the stereo conversation
Compression to keep it behind the main bass lane
High-pass EQ
Long reverb and delay sends
Rhythmic ducking to stay out of the stab rhythm
EQ Eight cleanup
Drive or transient emphasis if the stab needs bite
Delay throws and tightly controlled reverb
EQ carve around the main stab lane
Short ambience send
Transient softening if it feels too pointy
High-pass and clip trimming
Delay/reverb send rather than heavy inserts
Duck or fade the transient if it jumps over the stab
Filter automation
Throw reverb or delay into section changes
Gain rides to keep transitions under control
High-pass filtering
Long spatial FX
Subtle ducking if the bed flattens the drop

returns.name: Tight Room
returns.purpose: Short placement for drums, chops, and small stabs.

returns.name: Long Plate/Hall
returns.purpose: Dark tail for pads, breakdowns, and emotional lift.

returns.name: Dub Delay
returns.purpose: Call-and-response throws on stabs or vocal snippets.

automation_rules: Use mutes and rhythmic dropouts to create tension before the groove returns.
Automate send lifts on the final half-bar into groove resets or switch-ups.
Reserve the biggest FX movement for the switch-up or final groove return.
mix_rules: Keep the bass anchor centered and make the reese or support layer carry the side energy.
If the stab owns the hook, the pad should stay wider and softer.
Do not fill every rhythmic gap with percussion; 2-step needs holes to groove.
Keep a vocal or sample lane open even if the track stays instrumental in the lesson.

export_plan.target_bounce: 24-bit WAV master and lesson reference bounce
export_plan.headroom_note: Keep enough pre-limiter headroom for the groove to stay punchy after mastering.
final_checks: 2-step syncopation is still obvious after FX and loop layers
Stab or hook remains the clear focal point
Break and switch-up both justify their bar count
Outro leaves a usable DJ exit
